name: C U P
description_short: CUP Federal Credit Union is a membership-based financial institution serving employees and families of the Bureau of Reclamation and affiliated water districts in Provo, Utah.
description_long:
CUP Federal Credit Union operates as a specialized credit union primarily serving a defined membership base: employees and family members of the Bureau of Reclamation, affiliated water districts, and other agencies housed in the Bureau of Reclamation building in Provo, Utah. The institution has been positioned as a member-focused alternative to traditional banking with an emphasis on personalized service within its eligible membership community.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including checking and savings accounts, online and mobile banking, debit cards with Visa functionality, direct deposit, and competitive lending products. Loan offerings include vehicle financing (new/used vehicles at 4.99% APR, RVs at 7.24% APR), signature loans (8.99% APR), and a 90-day payment deferment promotion on auto loans. Members can also access investment options and fund transfer services. The organization emphasizes convenience through online account enrollment and anytime, anywhere account access.

CUP FCU distinguishes itself through its restricted membership model, which creates a tightly-knit member community with shared employment or family connections to specific federal agencies. The credit union actively engages members through community initiatives, including annual $500 scholarship programs. Operating hours are traditional business hours (Monday-Friday, 10 AM-5 PM) with a single physical location in Provo, Utah. The organization maintains direct communication channels including phone, fax, email, and text-based support.

For consumers within the eligible membership group, CUP FCU provides competitive rates and personalized service typical of credit unions. However, the strict membership eligibility requirement significantly limits accessibility. Potential members must verify employment or family relationship with qualifying organizations before applying for any services. The single physical location and limited weekend hours may inconvenience some members despite online banking availability.
services: Online account enrollment and digital banking
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.99% APR for new/used vehicles
Low-cost membership entry with only $25 opening deposit required
Online and mobile banking available for anytime account access
90-day payment deferral promotion on car loans reduces initial payment burden
Community-focused with annual $500 scholarship program for member families
Multiple contact options including phone, fax, email, and text support
Member-owned structure typical of credit unions may result in better rates than banks
cons: Strict membership eligibility limited to Bureau of Reclamation employees/families and affiliated water district employees, excluding most consumers
Single physical location in Provo, Utah with no branches limits accessibility for non-local members
Limited hours (Monday-Friday 10 AM-5 PM, closed weekends) restricts in-person service availability
No mention of credit-building products, credit monitoring, or credit repair services
Signature loan rates at 8.99% APR are higher than many competing credit unions and online lenders
best_for: Bureau of Reclamation employees seeking competitive rates within their employee credit union
Families of eligible federal water district workers wanting member-owned banking benefits
Provo, Utah area residents with qualifying employment seeking personalized local credit union service
Borrowers needing auto financing with payment deferral options through promotional periods
diagnosis: CUP Federal Credit Union is exclusively designed for employees and family members of the Bureau of Reclamation and affiliated water districts—a highly restricted membership base that disqualifies the vast majority of consumers. While the credit union offers competitive rates and personalized service typical of quality credit unions, prospective members must first verify their employment or family eligibility with specific federal agencies before any account or loan applications are possible.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: C U P — Credit Union in Provo, UT | CreditDoc
meta_description: C U P — NCUA-insured credit union in Provo, UT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
